summary: RIDEMIND Spot Finder quiver+target_date/hour update tested end-to-end. Backend: 23/23 pytest tests pass (added 6 new tests for quiver schema, empty quiver, past date clamping, +30d future date clamping, old-schema backwards compat, plus expanded markdown-stripping assertions on /spot-recommend ai_advice and /video-analysis structured fields). Frontend: Spot Finder UI verified - QUIVER chips section with default [9m,12m], add via Enter and AJOUTER button both work, × removes a kite, date input has min=today/max=today+14d, hour slider 6-20 default 14, old kite-input and wind-input correctly removed. Submitted form -> 3 spots shown each with 'Kite conseillé: Xm' badge (12m picked from quiver of [7,9,12]); AI advice (CONSEIL IA panel) is plain prose, no markdown chars (# * ** _ `).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~810 lines - past the 700-line split threshold. Strongly recommend extracting auth, checkout, video-analysis, spots routers into separate modules.
best_kite() heuristic uses ideal = 600/max(8, wind_kts) * (weight/75). With wind <8 kts it clamps to 8 (so ideal is overestimated) - acceptable but worth documenting.

Open-Meteo hourly indexing assumes returned array starts at 00:00 local for the requested start_date when timezone=auto. This held in tests but if Open-Meteo ever shifts (DST edge), wind_at_hour could be off by 1h. Safer: look up by 'time' label.
CORS still uses allow_origins='*' with allow_credentials=True (flagged previously) - browsers reject this combo for credentialed requests.
strip_markdown() uses __import__('re') inline at module load (works, but unconventional). Cleaner to just `import re` at top.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (quiver chips, date+hour pickers, submit -> Kite conseillé badge, markdown-free AI advice)

seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Reused premium user; standard user re-created on-the-fly via mongosh in test_spot_recommend_standard_forbidden.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests file /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py now uses the new quiver/target_date/target_hour schema. The 6 new quiver-related tests + 1 old-schema-compat test all pass. Markdown stripping is verified on both /spot-recommend ai_advice and /video-analysis structured fields. Frontend Spot Finder displays chips uppercase ('9M', '12M') due to font-display CSS - case-insensitive comparison required in playwright scripts. recommended_kite reliably picked from quiver (e.g. 12m for ~15-20 kts, 75kg).
rca of the issue: N/A - all new features (quiver array, target_date/target_hour clamping, markdown stripping on AI outputs) work as specified.
